positive.prompt: Review this pull request before I merge it
Before I merge this branch into main, do a five-axis readiness review of its diff
Is this pull request ready to land in the release branch after an independent review?

negative.prompt: Help me refine a vague product idea
Write a specification before implementation begins

evals.prompt: Review the complete branch diff before merging it into main. It adds a user-search endpoint. Deliver a merge-readiness decision.
evals.expected_output: A read-only multi-axis merge-readiness decision with severity-labelled findings, file:line references, and the reviewed merge range
expectations: The response establishes the target branch and reviews the complete branch-to-target range rather than only the latest commit
Findings cover more than one axis (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance)
Every finding carries a severity label from the skill's taxonomy
Security of user input is explicitly considered for the new endpoint
The review leads with high-leverage findings rather than nits
The response returns APPROVE, CHANGES_REQUESTED, or BLOCKED without editing code

evals.prompt: Do a general code-quality pass on this module. I am not deciding whether to merge a branch.
evals.expected_output: A concise boundary response that does not perform a merge-readiness review or edit code without a merge decision
expectations: The response states that the skill is limited to pre-merge branch or pull-request decisions
The response does not invent a merge target or review range
The response does not produce a merge verdict or edit code
